course_title: Critical Thinking & Scientific Writing in Animal & Veterinary Biosciences
course_description:
The content of the course will depend on the area of study selected and the format of the study negotiated with the Honours supervisors. You can expect to study a number of issues as core content which can be presented using one or more teaching methods including but not limited to, lectures, seminars, group discussions, case studies, collating and reviewing the literature and other self-directed learning.

Producing concise, relevant, and scientifically robust Literature Reviews is a fundamental skill for researchers. This course provides students with the opportunity to develop a detailed Literature Review on a topic directly related to their Honours Research Project. In addition to building skills in academic writing, the Literature Review helps Students become familiar with the key theoretical and technical concepts that underpin their Research Project. It also enables the Honours Supervisor to provide early feedback on writing style before Students begin drafting their Thesis.

The Literature Review topic should offer essential background knowledge for the Honours Research Project - knowledge the Student is unlikely to have gained during their undergraduate studies. The VETS6640 Literature Review should complement, but not duplicate, the literature review included in the Honours Research Project Thesis. While a condensed and refocused version of the VETS6640 Literature Review may be incorporated into the Thesis, the two documents should not be identical.

Students are required to prepare and submit their Literature Review by the end of their first semester of enrolment. Completing the Literature Review at this stage supports the development of the Honours Research Project Thesis and strengthens the Student’s ability to critically analyse scientific literature. Submission at the end of Semester 1 also allows Students to receive valuable feedback on their academic writing and overall progress at the mid‑candidature point.

companions: You'll need to complete the following courses at the same time:

timetable:
Additional timetable information
VETS6640 is entirely self-directed learning, which will require:
the development of a learning contract following discussion with your supervisor(s);
the regular (weekly is advised) scheduling of meetings with your supervisor(s), and
the composition and submission of a literature review.
Please check your course Blackboard site frequently for the latest Timetable information.
Please note that unexpected changes to the Timetable may occur throughout the Semester. Therefore, it is crucial to regularly check the course Blackboard site. Any updates to the timetable will be communicated to students via Blackboard Announcements.
course_aims: This course aims to allow you to develop skills in independent research within an area related to your Thesis. You will learn how to communicate science through the compilation of a Literature Review. During the process, you will learn to follow scientific protocols when writing, develop a deeper understanding of your selected topic, question the validity of new information, critically evaluate work and establish valid conclusions.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate a structured, evidence-based approach to scientific writing, ensuring relevant literature citation and organisation of arguments.
Show evidence of a developing command and understanding of relevant concepts and disciplines.
Apply increasing understanding of your topic to refining and developing your literature review.
Analyse and evaluate a broad range of literature on your topic and draw objective conclusions and/or identify gaps in current knowledge.
Professionally present the literature review in terms of style, formatting, presenting relevant tables and figures and editing, to a standard appropriate for Journal submission.

assessment_details.title: Postgraduate Advanced Topic Literature Review
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Honours students must prepare and submit a Literature Review on a topic that is related to their research project by the end of their first semester of enrolment, as the Literature Review assists in the development of the Thesis. The format of the Literature Review may vary according to the nature of the field (experimental, theoretical, comparative, descriptive, etc), but its purpose is the same in all fields. It should be on a topic that is relevant to the Honours research question being asked in VETS6616, VETS6617, VETS6628 or VETS6629, but should not be the same as the actual Literature Review of the Honours Thesis. For example, the topic could be related to a particular set of methodologies that are required to conduct the research project, such as hormone assays or nutritional analyses. The review should summarise salient findings, arguments, theories and other developments. This assessment task is linked with the Honours Assessment Authenticity Report. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your Assessment through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard.
Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin, please name your file in the following way:
For example, SMITH Bob_54329876_VETS1234_Essay1
All students must ensure they receive their Turnitin receipt on submission of any assessments. A valid Turnitin receipt will be the only evidence accepted if one or more of your submissions are missing. Without evidence, the assessment will receive the standard late penalty or, after seven days, will receive zero. In the case of a Blackboard outage, please contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible to confirm the outage with ITS.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students with acceptable reasons (medical or compassionate reasons or as a result of exceptional circumstances) may apply for an extension of the deadline. Information on applying for an extension can be found here: my.UQ Applying for an extension .
Extension applications must be received by the assessment due date and time.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).
10% will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point your submission will receive a mark of zero (0) unless an extension has been approved.

assessment_details.title: Honours Assessment Authenticity Report
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The template for this report can be found on Blackboard. Task instructions The Honours Authenticity Report must be submitted by the principal supervisor during the week prior to the Literature Review submission. This is a Pass/Fail hurdle assessment item. To pass, students must demonstrate to their supervisor a detailed understanding of their written submission. The report, combined with supervisor oversight, is intended to verify and document the student’s comprehension of the topic and scientific principles in their Literature Review. Supervisor Sign-Off and Support As this is a Pass/Fail assessment, if a supervisor is unwilling to sign the authenticity report, the student must provide evidence to justify an override by the Honours Program Coordinator. Students are advised to: Keep minutes from supervisory meetings. Save copies of draft documents at each stage using UQ’s Research Data Manager (RDM). If a student feels they are not receiving adequate or agreed-upon support from their supervisor, they should contact the Honours Coordinator at f.shapter@uq.edu.au as soon as possible. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The supervisor will email the report to the course coordinator.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
If a student has an approved extension for the Literature Review assessment task, the same extension will automatically be applied to this assessment item.
Students with acceptable reasons (medical or compassionate reasons or as a result of exceptional circumstances) may apply for an extension of the deadline. Information on applying for an extension can be found here: my.UQ Applying for an extension .
Extension applications must be received by the assessment due date and time.

assessment_details.title: Outline of Literature Review
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The task is to submit a Literature Review outline (Part A) and Workflow Plan (Part B). This will be worth 20% of the final mark for VETS6640. Please ensure you have read the full task description for the Literature Review assessment Task (55%) before undertaking this assessment. This task is a planning tool for the larger written task. The Outline (Part A) 15% 1. Follow the recommended literature review outline structure and format in the SVS Hons Handbook, available on Blackboard 2. Prepare 1-2 paragraphs of a referenced, well-written introduction to set the scope and background. 3. Prepare a summary (bullet points are acceptable in the outline document) summarising the content of each subsequent section (excluding references). 4. The outline should be NO MORE than two pages. 5. Outline formatting should be the same as the final review (font, line spacing, headings and subheading structure). The Workflow Plan (Part B) 5% Should be an agreed workflow for the semester between the supervisor and student, noting critical milestones to be achieved against the weeks of the semester. This should be presented as a Gantt chart, with a table describing each milestone using the SMART goals format as a template for writing the milestone statement. The workplan should include scheduled, regular meetings with supervisors (suggest weekly as a minimum) and be aligned and integrated with the development of the sections of the literature review identified in the outline document. Templates will be provided on Blackboard in the resources folder. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Please refer to the SVS Hons Handbook, available on Blackboard, for more details of this task and the marking rubric. Read full task description Hide full task description

A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time.
If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).
10% will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point your submission will receive a mark of zero (0) unless an extension has been approved.

assessment_details.title: Supervisor Evaluation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The Supervisor Evaluation form is available in the SVS Honours Handbook and on Blackboard, and should be viewed together with the supervisor(s) and students within week one of candidature. This is to be undertaken so all parties are clear about the marking criteria, as this is a semester-long evaluation of student performance and growth as an independent researcher. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
To be completed and emailed to the Course Coordinator by the Supervisor(s), on the due date for the literature review.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Any extension granted to the student's candidature, will automatically be applied to the supervisor's evaluation due date, to ensure the entire candidature has been assessed.
Students with acceptable reasons (medical or compassionate reasons or as a result of exceptional circumstances) may apply for an extension of the deadline. Information on applying for an extension can be found here: my.UQ Applying for an extension .
Extension applications must be received by the assessment due date and time.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Prepare an outline document summarising the literature review See assessment task description
Extensive and in-depth literature review and discussions
Prepare iterative drafts of scientific literature review and receive supervisor input
Finalise documents for submission after Supervisor feedback on Drafts

Review the Assessment Authenticity & Supervisor Evaluation forms collaboratively The Assessment Authenticity and Supervisor Evaluation reports must be viewed and discussed with your supervisor during week one. These forms must be completed by your Supervisor at the end of the semester to pass this course.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Fitness to Practise Policy Vaccinations and Immunisation Procedure Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Course guidelines Please access the SVS Honours Guidelines Handbook in your course Blackboard's learning resources, for detailed information on all aspects of your candidature in the BSc (Gatton) Hons program.
